name: Financial Report Generation Workflow
description: Gather financial data, perform analysis, and produce a structured financial report with commentary

inputs.name: report_period
inputs.description: Time period the report covers (e.g. Q1 2026, March 2026)

inputs.name: data_source
inputs.description: Path or location of the financial data files (CSV, JSON, or spreadsheet)

inputs.name: report_type
inputs.description: Type of report: P&L, balance sheet, cash flow, or budget vs actuals

inputs.name: audience
inputs.description: Intended audience: executive, board, finance team, or external

inputs.name: comparison_period
inputs.description: Prior period for variance analysis (e.g. Q4 2025)

outputs.name: financial_report
outputs.description: Structured report document with summary, tables, and narrative commentary

outputs.name: variance_analysis
outputs.description: Period-over-period variance table with explanations for significant movements

outputs.name: executive_summary
outputs.description: One-paragraph headline summary suitable for senior stakeholders

steps: Confirm the report period, data source, report type, and audience with the user
Read and validate the financial data files; flag any missing or anomalous values

then: Compute period-over-period variances for all key line items
Identify the top three positive and negative variance drivers
else: Compute totals and key ratios for the current period only

steps: Structure the report body: header, executive summary, detailed tables, and narrative commentary

then: Lead with the executive summary and key headlines before the detailed tables
Use plain language and avoid internal jargon
else: Include full line-item detail and methodology notes for the finance team

steps: Draft narrative commentary explaining performance drivers, risks, and outlook
Present the draft report to the user for review

then: Apply the requested changes and re-present the updated report
